title: Living in Webster Groves, MO
content:
Webster Groves is an affluent suburb ~2 miles west of St. Louis within the I-270 ring (zip 63119). Named Missouri's safest city in 2021.

PROXIMITY: Downtown 15 min, Forest Park 12 min, Delmar Loop 15 min. Metrolink light rail access.

HOUSING & REAL ESTATE:
- Median sale price: $475,000
- Median price/sq ft: $254
- Average days on market: 5 days (seller's market)
- 20th most expensive St. Louis suburb
- Five historic districts with architectural diversity: Queen Anne, Tudor Revival, Italianate, Colonial Revival, Bungalow
- New construction must align with historic aesthetics

HISTORIC NEIGHBORHOODS: Webster, Old Orchard, Webster Park, Tuxedo Park, Selma

EDUCATION:
- Webster Groves School District: 'A' overall on Niche, teachers rated 'A+'
- Webster Groves High School: 2nd oldest public HS west of the Mississippi
- Turkey Day Game vs Kirkwood: ESPN and Sports Illustrated featured rivalry
- Webster University, Eden Theological Seminary
- Private: Mary Queen of Peace, Holy Redeemer, The College School

DEMOGRAPHICS:
- Population: 23,771, density 4,020/sq mi
- Median age: 40.3, avg household 2.4
- Median household income: $110,716
- 69% hold bachelor's or graduate degree
- Cost of living 13% above national average

DINING: Balkan Treat Box, Frisco Ballroom, The Parkmoor Drive-In, Le Macaron, The Clover and the Bee, Olive and Oak
SHOPPING: Civil Alchemy, Union Studio, Webster Groves Bookshop (since 1965), The Hub Bicycle Company

ARTS & CULTURE:
- Opera Theatre of Saint Louis, The Repertory Theatre
- Webster Arts Fair (15,000+ visitors), Old Webster Jazz and Blues Festival (12,000 attendees)
- Webster Wonderland, Community Days Parade

PARKS: 21 parks, 120+ acres. Blackburn Park, Larson Park, Webster Sculpture Park. Walk Score downtown: 81.
